reply:
[[BLOCK:slide69_shape1]]
Empirical research has confirmed the superiority of Generalized Regression (广义回归 (Generalized Regression), GR).  
A cohort study (Kan, Kharrazi, Chang, Bodycombe, Lemke, & Weiner, 2019) included 81,106 Medicare Advantage patients who had continuous medical and pharmacy insurance coverage from 2009 to 2013. The study used comorbidity indicators from 2009 to 2012 to predict total medical expenses in 2013.  
Using 2012 data, Ordinary Least Squares Regression (OLS Regression) (普通最小二乘回归 (OLS Regression)) performed poorly (e.g., R-squared (R平方 (R Square), R2) = 16.3%), whereas Penalized Regression (惩罚回归 (Penalized Regression)) models achieved R-squared values ranging from 16.8% to 16.9%.  
When using predictors from 2009 to 2012, the performance gap widened further (R-squared: 15.0% versus 18.0%–18.2%).
